programme.desc: Pilot episode of the crime drama, starring Amanda Redman as Superintendent Sandra Pullman, a rising star of the Met who is sidelined after a failed hostage rescue. Put in charge of a new department, she is dismayed to discover the unit is to be manned by a motley crew of old-school former detectives who know little about modern police procedure. However, when they re-examine the case of a man whose conviction for murder 20 years ago has just been overturned, their combined experience and unorthodox methods bring interesting results. In the Locksbottom branch in Kent, Halloween could end up being a bit of a nightmare. The store has a low customer satisfaction rating, so manager Mij is on a mission to retrain and re-invigorate his young team in preparation for the big pumpkin push. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.title: Election 2019
programme.desc: Huw Edwards takes the helm for the BBC's comprehensive overnight coverage as the results of the General Election are announced, joined by Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Faisal Islam and Amol Rajan, along with Professor John Curtice. Sophie Raworth analyses the results on a giant map of the UK, Sarah Smith and Kirsty Wark broadcast live from Scotland, and correspondents including Naga Munchetty, Andrew Marr, Martha Kearney, Nick Robinson and Lucy Manning will have news and results from key constituency locations across the country. Finally, the exit poll - and the eventual result - will be projected onto Old Broadcasting House, providing a clear idea of who will be leading the country over the next five years(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Return of the programme following life at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, Shetland. A Norwegian sailor is brought in after fracturing his ankle aboard his ship, while new junior doctor Freddie Searight must dust off his French to treat an Indonesian fisherman who comes into A&E with an eye injury(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a well-travelled leather case, a home-made telescope built from scrap metal and a 100-year-old bowl with an extraordinary history(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by rail through Queensland, marvelling at the engineering expertise of a railway line built in the 1890s. He visits a plantation where he learns how to harvest sugar cane, finds out how the townsfolk of Innisfail withstand the tropical storms that strike the region and ventures into the hinterland, where he braves a crocodile safari. At Mungalla Cattle Station, he hears how aboriginal people faced brutality at the hands of 19th-century settlers and after a singalong, he makes tracks for his end destination of Townsville - the gateway to the Great Barrier Reef(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of classic recipes from the BBC archives, focusing on classic winter warmers that work equally well in spring, autumn and summer. The menu includes Keith Floyd's beef provencale, a chicken dumpling soup from Rachel Khoo, the Hairy Bikers' apple pie and James Martin working wonders with the humble jacket potato(n)

programme.title: The Best Christmas Food Ever
programme.desc: Paul Ainsworth and Catherine Fulvio are joined by actor Christopher Biggins as they share culinary secrets, perfect for creating delicious festive flavours for Christmas(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's Len Goodman takes dancer Anton du Beke back to Blackpool to reminisce about a seaside holiday in 1981, including taking him for a tide on a vintage tram(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Anne Reid is keen to find out about her father's family, but only has a couple of clues to go on - the name of a house in Scotland and an old story that his ancestors were ministers in the church. This puts her on the trail of her great-great-grandfather John Reid in Fife, who turns out to have been a teacher and not a minister. However, he also had a less-honest sideline which takes the story in an altogether different direction(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers create festive dishes with the help of The One Show's Alex Jones, including a turkey salad, figs stuffed with stilton, and a chocolate and chestnut roulade(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Ulster Folk and Transport Museum in Belfast, and pays a visit to Europe's last water-powered spade mill. With experts Mark Stacey and Caroline Hawley(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal talks to the couple who won the last spot in the Strictly final, and previews the songs the couples will be dancing to on Saturday in Tunes on Tuesday(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Hollyoaks stars Will Mellor and Nick Pickard hunt for bargains in north west England, assisted by experts Paul Laidlaw and Steph Connell. Along the way, they uncover the unusual history of Merseyside village Port Sunlight and learn about glass-making, before bringing their finds - a pocket watch and a ship's porthole - to Greenwich to sell at auction(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace presents the first round of semi-finals week. The eight remaining contestants face two challenges, to be judged by Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing. Firstly, they must prepare a dish that captures the moment that triggered their own love of food. After this, they move on to the invention test, in which they must make a flawless vegan meal(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef concludes his tour with trips to the Languedoc and Provence. In the border town of Uzes, Rick samples salt cod and marvels at a colourful array of French patisserie - selecting a seasonal fig tart to have with his morning coffee. He moves on to the harbour town of Cassis to try North African lamb chorba cooked by a local fisherman, while back in his country kitchen, he prepares a confit tomato and aubergine tarte tatin(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Desiree Burch hosts the show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing stand-up performances by Paul McCaffrey and Jonny Pelham(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the 14th round of fixtures, including Los Angeles Rams v Seattle Seahawks(n)

programme.title: The New Girlfriend (2014)
programme.desc: A woman grieving the death of her lifelong best friend gets to know her husband, only to discover he secretly likes wearing his late wife's clothes. She helps him to accept his transvestism, and teaches him to create a new female alter ego for himself. Drama based on a short story by Ruth Rendell, starring Anais Demoustier and Romain Duris. In French(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: Documentary series following families throughout their first weeks of parenthood, lifting the lid on one of the most joyful, yet intense, times in a family's life. The opening episode introduces the first set of the six expectant families featured in the series, including young first-time parents Syler and Mo Nicholson-Bayoumi from Nottinghamshire, and Helen and Nigel Pierce, who are expecting their fourth child(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste track down a man who made off with a woman's savings, and look at how social media profiles and pictures can be hijacked by criminals(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A farmer and his daughter are both rushed to A&E with injuries just as their family is busy with sheep-shearing season, and a crofter has to have a kidney removed(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a family butcher's trusty sausage-making machine, a treasured Edwardian postcard album, and an Iraqi hot water urn, known as a samovar(n)

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Footage of emergency services personnel assaulted in the line of duty. An arrest in Edinburgh escalates, resulting in an officer severing an artery. Meanwhile, PC Ian Bruce, who is part of a specialist team tackling vehicle crime, finds himself alone and vulnerable after members of a gang he is chasing turn on him(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn head to Ferryside, a close-knit coastal community just eight miles from Carmarthen in south-west Wales. They want to celebrate the work and achievements of Ferryside Lifeboat, a completely independent organisation run entirely by volunteers, and prepare a spectacular fishy feast, cooked in a giant fire pit on the beach(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of homemade meals to sate cravings for a takeaway, including fish and chips from James Martin and a pizza recipe from Keith Floyd(n)

programme.title: The Best Christmas Food Ever
programme.desc: Paul Ainsworth and Catherine Fulvio are joined by Jay Blades as they share festive recipes including pheasant breast with wild mushrooms and spiced poached pears(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: The Strictly Come Dancing judge takes Homes Under the Hammer presenter Lucy Alexander back to Herne Bay in Kent to reminisce about a holiday she had in 1979(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: BBC news correspondent Frank Gardner sets out to find out whether there is any truth in his late mother's claim that her side of the family arrived in Britain with the Normans. Along the way, he traces his ancestry back to his 10-time great-grandfather Sir Michael Stanhope - a knight of the Tudor court, who was accused of treason. But the journey does not end there, as he gets much closer to his mother's suspicions than he could ever have imagined(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Antiques expert Paul Martin joins the duo as they cook meat-free festive recipes, including sage and spinach gnocchi, nut roast with all the trimmings and a hazelnut and pear cake(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball shares news and gossip from Strictly Come Dancing as one of the final couples joins her on the sofa. Plus, Ian Waite casts his eye over the latest training footage(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests with interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first three remaining semi-finalists battle it out for a place in final, and are given the chance to work alongside one of the UK's most exciting chefs, Gareth Ward. First, the chefs are challenged to create a dish of their own design for Gareth to taste, inspired by his own cooking. The competitors will then take on the daunting task of joining Gareth's brigade, taking responsibility for two dishes each during a highly-pressurised service, before returning to the MasterChef kitchen to present a dish of their own design to the judges(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: Documentary series following families throughout their first weeks of parenthood, lifting the lid on one of the most joyful, yet intense, times in a family's life. In the third episode, the families find new ways to cope as the dads go back to work. New dad Mo feels pressure to earn more cash, while Nigel is a little too glad of the break from his family of seven. Hermisha does not want Shabazz's paternity leave to end, and Paul and Craig say an emotional goodbye to Mel as they leave the hospital to begin their new life with the twins(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice, with Tom Allen talking to the latest candidates to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of the interviews(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: The Edge of Seventeen (2016)
programme.desc: High-school life becomes even more unbearable for Nadine when her best friend, Krista, starts dating her older brother. Feeling more alone than ever, Nadine strikes up an unexpected friendship with Erwin, which gives her a glimmer of hope that life as a high-school junior may not be so bad after all. Coming-of-age comedy drama, starring Hailee Steinfeld, Haley Lu Richardson, Blake Jenner, Hayden Szeto and Woody Harrelson(n)

programme.title: Reggie in China
programme.desc: Reggie Yates travels to China and visits four very different cities. His first stop is Shenzhen, which over the past 40 years has grown from a region dominated by fishing and farmland to a bustling metropolis that is home to 12 million people and at the centre of the country's technological revolution. He meets the inventors of an earpiece that uses algorithms to translate speech into other languages, discovers more about the differing attitudes between generations, and witnesses a demonstration of a flying motorcycle(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: There is excitement in the air as the Sainsbury's staff prepare for a party to celebrate the supermarket's 150th year of trading, especially as the Queen will be there. After 50 years' service on the tills, Jenny from the Wallington store has been selected to meet Her Royal Highness and she cannot wait. As the supermarket celebrates its past, it also looks to the future by trialling an experiment to remove all the checkouts from one store, encouraging customers to pay with their mobiles. Will it change the face of shopping or simply send customers elsewhere?(n)

programme.title: Novels That Shaped Our World
programme.desc: The documentary explores how novels through the ages have embraced the class struggle, from Dickens, Gaskell and Hardy through to Robert Tressell's The Ragged-Trousered Philanthropists and the group of working-class writers who began to write their own stories in post-war Britain. With readings by Russell Tovey, Darren Boyd, Alex Macqueen, Emily Berrington, Freddie Fox, Rian Gordon and Olivia Barrowclough(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: An oil rig worker from Tyne and Wear is rushed to hospital with a suspected stroke. Meanwhile, medics and emergency service staff raise money for an MRI scanner for Shetland(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life. Today, the team repair a shattered gold ring, a dilapidated fire bucket, and a jewellery box(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison compete to win a commission to transform a garden room in a three-bedroom Victorian terraced house in Shrewsbury for Mike and Lisa. Robert suggests a structure that gives each of them their own separate spaces, while Laura attempts to blend the needs of the whole family with in a highly adaptable garden room(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives, focusing on dishes that all have something of a kick to them(n)

programme.title: The Best Christmas Food Ever
programme.desc: Paul Ainsworth and Catherine Fulvio are joined by DJ Dev Griffin as they rustle up roast garlic-infused baked camembert, blueberry cinnamon swirls and spicy vanilla rice pudding(n)

programme.title: The Best Christmas Food Ever
programme.desc: Paul Ainsworth and Catherine Fulvio are joined by Debbie McGee as they rustle up slow-cooked duck, a duo of soups and warm chocolate cake with sweet clementine cheese(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor and writer Mark Gatiss has always had a love of storytelling, especially for the ghastly and the ghoulish, but has always wondered where it comes from. Setting out to research his ancestry, he is intrigued to discover that his mother's side of the family goes back five generations to the wilds of Northern Ireland. Travelling over there, he untangles a tale of rags-to-riches and learns that sure enough, his family were indeed story-tellers - but fascinatingly, they were also said to be vampire-slayers(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Denise Lewis joins the duo as they create a trio of canapes, porcetta pork loin and a prawn and salmon terrine. The retired athlete also explains how she spends her Christmas. Alongside the recipes and fun there are also fantastic ideas for homemade edible Christmas presents(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams test their general knowledge skills on this week's special House of Games: Champion editions(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball is joined by the finalists' friends and family, who share their thoughts on how their loved ones will do, and designer Vicky Gill reveals some of Saturday night's costumes(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Russell Kane and presenter AJ Odudu navigate their way around Shropshire looking for antiques, accompanied by experts Phil Serrell and Margie Cooper. Eventually they head to an auction to discover whose haul raises the most money(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The last semi-final sees the remaining hopefuls head to Ollie Dabbous's London restaurant Hide, where they receive a masterclass from the Michelin-starred chef in making his beef tartare. He then asks them to create a dish from the restaurant's select larder before they are each given responsibility for one of the dishes during a packed service. Back at the studio, a final test awaits in which the hopefuls must cook two dishes for Gregg Wallace, Marcus Wareing and Monica Galetti, who decide who will go through to next week's finals(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Cherry Healey find out how favourite festive party foods are made. Gregg visits an enormous factory in Nottingham where 200,000 canapes are produced every 24 hours. Cherry discovers fail-safe scientific methods for cooking the perfect Christmas roast, turning her oven into a turkey sauna by popping ice cubes into a baking tray. Historian Ruth Goodman looks into the tradition of kissing under the mistletoe and also investigates how the period of advent, traditionally associated with fasting, came to be connected with chocolate-packed calendars(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig has a Christmas knees-up in the pub with Alan Davies, Cariad Lloyd, Josh Widdicombe and Noel Fielding. Pub-themed questions are the order of the day, with a sprinkling of festive cheer along the way(n)

programme.title: I Give It a Year (2013)
programme.desc: A couple marry after a whirlwind romance, but family and friends have doubts about the relationship. As their first anniversary approaches, they both begin to wonder if they are right for each other, especially when faced with more appealing alternative partners. Romantic comedy, with Rose Byrne, Rafe Spall, Anna Faris and Simon Baker(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as Westminster University take on the University of East Anglia. Writer and presenter Danny Wallace and Royal Institution Christmas lecturer Sophie Scott are on the Westminster team, while comedian Arthur Smith and weather presenter Darren Bett are on the opposing side(n)

programme.title: Secret Life of Farm Animals
programme.desc: The documentary follows a litter of piglets from birth as they grow up in the Brecon Beacons. The theory that every piglet always returns to the same teat to suckle is tested, while facts such as pigs love mud to keep cool because they have practically no sweat glands are revealed. The animals' intelligence is demonstrated with a series of puzzles, and it is shown the species can be masters at the art of deception. Plus, a farm that uses llamas to guard its sheep and a pet rabbit with a remarkable identity crisis(n)

programme.title: Wild Weather with Richard Hammond
programme.desc: The host investigates the importance of heat - or the lack of it - when it comes to weather. And it isn't just a load of hot air. Without temperature, clouds, rain, snow, dust storms, thunder or lightning would be unable to form. Along the way he builds his own massive dust storm, discovering how sand from the Sahara bounces its way to the UK, and also drops in on a scientist who creates lightning bolts in his laboratory(n)

programme.title: Rome Unpacked
programme.desc: Chef Giorgio Locatelli and art critic Andrew Graham-Dixon conclude their exploration of Italy's capital. In the second of two programmes, as well as marvelling at the mosaics in the 12th-century Basilica di San Clemente, Andrew takes Giorgio to an ancient Roman schoolteacher's classroom. Then it's on to a true architectural and civic wonder - the vast Testaccio Slaughterhouse, where workers were once paid in offal. Travelling to the Palazzo Colonna, Andrew shows Giorgio the Beaneater by Carracci, a Baroque painting that makes an everyday subject extraordinary(n)

programme.title: Looking for Rembrandt
programme.desc: The story behind Rembrandt's most iconic work, The Night Watch. The events that surrounded the creation of his masterpiece contributed to a 10-year decline in his output, as he devoted his energies to a series of etchings inspired by his extra-marital affairs, while a struggle to manage his debts led him to declare bankruptcy(n)

programme.title: Oceans Apart: Art and the Pacific with James Fox
programme.desc: The art historian traces the effects of the West's contact with the peoples and cultures of the Pacific, beginning with the impact on the Aborigines of Captain Cook's arrival in Australia in 1770. He reveals how the indigenous people were almost destroyed by European colonisation, but held on to their art to survive, flourish and ultimately share their extraordinary culture with the world(n)

programme.title: The Road to Palmyra
programme.desc: Historian Dan Cruickshank and photographer Don McCullin venture into the heart of war-torn Syria on a dangerous mission to document the cultural destruction wrought by Isis(n)

programme.title: Revolution and Romance: Musical Masters of the 19th Century
programme.desc: Suzy Klein's odyssey through the history of music in the 19th century concludes, turning the spotlight on later years, when technological revolutions seemed to make the world a smaller, more accessible place. Focusing on the Paris Exposition of 1889, a cultural showcase celebrating the centenary of the French Revoution, she reveals how composers such as Claude Debussy were exposed to the sounds of the East - immediately breaking Wagner's stranglehold of influence over Western music. She also examines the invention of saxophone, and makes her own recording on a vintage phonograph - an early precursor of vinyl records, cassette tapes and CDs(n)

programme.title: Secret Life of Farm Animals
programme.desc: The documentary follows a litter of piglets from birth as they grow up in the Brecon Beacons. The theory that every piglet always returns to the same teat to suckle is tested, while facts such as pigs love mud to keep cool because they have practically no sweat glands are revealed. The animals' intelligence is demonstrated with a series of puzzles, and it is shown the species can be masters at the art of deception. Plus, a farm that uses llamas to guard its sheep and a pet rabbit with a remarkable identity crisis(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as teams of distinguished graduates from Pembroke College in Cambridge and King's College in London compete in a festive edition(n)

programme.title: Hidden Killers
programme.desc: Dr Suzannah Lipscomb reveals some of the unexpected dangers lurking in the ever-changing homes of Britain in the 1950s. For the first time, moulded plywood furniture, fibreglass, plastics and polyester were becoming fixtures of domestic life, having all been developed and created during the Second World War. However, even as this bright new era of social change progressed, bringing higher living standards and improved technologies, new, unprecedented hazards were finding their way into everyday life(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team follow the search for artefacts from the Second World War, joining marine archaeologists in the Solent as they raise a Fairey Barracuda divebomber. Elsewhere, a dig in the Lake District tells the moving story of the Windermere Boys, and in Aldbourne in Wiltshire, archaeologists search for personal items from the most famous American unit of the US army, 'Easy Company', who were stationed there in 1943 and 1944(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Vic presents Bob with a striking new wig, before revealing his secret beat boxing skills. Plus, the return of the Man with the Stick, and sketches including the Free Runners and Frank Roddam's latest inventions. Comedy series with Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer, featuring sketches, slapstick, songs and stunts, accompanied by a series of special guests.(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: The pair fish at Christchurch's quintessential Bridge Pool in Dorset, hoping to catch sea trout, before Paul persuades Bob to charter a boat in search of sea bass. They venture out to the Needles where Paul makes a incredible catch, and decide to take the fish to a local restaurant to prepare it(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: In the final episode, Paul and Bob fish for Pike on the River Test in Hampshire, which may not be the best idea for two men of a certain age with heart problems. In the cottage where the pair are staying, they decide to invite a consultant cardiologist for dinner to talk about their medical past and their future, gaining tips on how to live the most healthy life possible. As the sun sets on the final night of their fishing expedition, Paul and Bob decide to write a eulogy for each on the riverbank(n)

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Exploring the forests, plains and bustling cities of Central Thailand, an area known as the nation's rice bowl. The programme explores how people live alongside animals in these areas, and the spiritual fulfilment to be found in these relationships. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo(n)

programme.title: Oceans Apart: Art and the Pacific with James Fox
programme.desc: Continuing his exploration of the collision of the West and Pacific culture, art historian Dr James Fox looks at how the West has created a vision of Polynesia as paradise. He travels across the Pacific to uncover the great sites and masterpieces of pre-contact Polynesian art, from the massive religious complex Taputapuatea on the island of Raiatea to the extraordinary feathered Ku heads from Hawaii, testament to the rich and sophisticated societies that once lived there(n)

programme.title: The High Art of the Low Countries
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the countries of Holland and Belgium, famous for their tulips and windmills, but which were actually forged through conflict and division. He examines how a period of economic prosperity, driven by the secular middle class, led to the Dutch Golden Age of the 17th century, during which time the concept of oil painting was conceived, and the master painters such as Rembrandt and Vermeer found success(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team follow the search for artefacts from the Second World War, joining marine archaeologists in the Solent as they raise a Fairey Barracuda divebomber. Elsewhere, a dig in the Lake District tells the moving story of the Windermere Boys, and in Aldbourne in Wiltshire, archaeologists search for personal items from the most famous American unit of the US army, 'Easy Company', who were stationed there in 1943 and 1944(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Alumni from Exeter University take on Birmingham University, with TV producer and executive Paul Jackson, news correspondent Jon Kay, comedian Chris Addison and actor Nigel Lindsay among the contestants. Presented by Jeremy Paxman(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Solar System
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox considers whether the building blocks of life might be found on other planets by studying the required substance that is most abundant on Earth - water. He descends to the bottom of the Pacific to discover the creatures that have evolved in this most alien of environments, and flies above the American Scablands to see the effect of the geological changes caused by water in exactly the circumstances astronomers believe occurred on Mars(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the popularity of train replicas, and how what started out as an adult obsession with building fully engineered model railways became the iconic toy of 1950s and 1960s childhood. With contributions from modellers such as Pete Waterman, this programme celebrates the joys of miniaturisation(n)

programme.title: The Secret Story of Stuff: Materials of the Modern Age
programme.desc: Engineer and designer Zoe Laughlin explores the world of material science, uncovering the fascinating innovations in manufacturing that are set to change the world we live in(n)

programme.title: Wild West: America's Great Frontier
programme.desc: Wildlife in America's mountain regions, including the Rockies and the Sierra Nevada, areas infamous for extremes of temperature from freezing winters to scorching summers. The programme examines the survival strategies employed by animals including black bears, pikas and bison, as well as parasitic plants. Plus, a look at the challenges faced by these region's human inhabitants, including ranchers leading their cattle on 70-mile journeys(n)

programme.desc: DW is excited to be taking care of the class bunny, Larry. But when Larry is feeling adventurous and wanders off, it's up to Ladonna, the bunny whisperer, to help get him back(n)

programme.title: Only Foals and Horses
programme.desc: Vets Lisa Durham and Philippa Hughes help to artificially inseminate a precious Arabian mare. programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.desc: Halloween presents one of the last big sales opportunities of the year, so the pressure is on Emma and Martin from the produce team, battling the wet weather to ensure this year's pumpkin crop will deliver the one million required for stores around the country. In the Locksbottom branch in Kent, Halloween could end up being a bit of a nightmare. The store has a low customer satisfaction rating, so manager Mij is on a mission to retrain and re-invigorate his young team in preparation for the big pumpkin push. programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and his team help the Newcombe family in Kent, who need to adapt their home to be able to care for their two-year-old son Arlo, who has condition that means his lungs must be constantly supported by breathing apparatus. Trailing breathing tubes, equipment the size of tumble dryers, and no downstairs bathroom have turned their once safe home into a risky space for the family. Nick and the team have amassed a huge group of volunteers for Arlo and the family, as they set to work creating a safe and inspiring space that Arlo and his family can call home(n)

programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits property developments in Wolverhampton and Croydon and Martin Roberts looks round three-bedroom semi-detached in Derby(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Newly qualified nurse Chloe is thrown in at the deep end treating a toddler who has difficulty breathing. A nightshift worker is flown in showing similar symptoms to a heart attack(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Entrepreneurs discover what customers think of their items - a secure drinks-carrying tray, a kit for making DIY beauty products and a lower-back pain-relief device(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara looks forward to Adam's return but finds herself trapped in a terrifying struggle with his unhinged twin brother Gareth(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Rob goes on the hunt for Gareth after his attack on Zara - so Adam tries to get to his evil twin before the police. Sid helps his friend Hayley, who has been having strange dreams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah goes property shopping in rural Devon with a couple who have a £350,000 budget and hope to find a home that has both a workshop and an art studio(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers meet a couple in Newport, Gwent, whose garden boasts dangerous sloped banks full of wild weeds, overgrown hedges and too many trees(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team takes on a smashed piggybank, a homemade gramophone that has lost its voice and a tarnished 19th-century German ceremonial helmet(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Having finally discovered the truth, Whitney finds herself in a dangerous situation, so Tiffany and Callum spring into action in an attempt to bring her to safety - will they be able to get to her in time? Ruby tells Max she is fed up of Ian - and decides it is time to teach the pompous restaurant owner a lesson. Jay offers Stuart a job and does his best to make it up to Lola(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Halloween presents one of the last big sales opportunities of the year, so the pressure is on Emma and Martin from the produce team, battling the wet weather to ensure this year's pumpkin crop will deliver the one million required for stores around the country. In the Locksbottom branch in Kent, Halloween could end up being a bit of a nightmare. The store has a low customer satisfaction rating, so manager Mij is on a mission to retrain and re-invigorate his young team in preparation for the big pumpkin push. Meanwhile, Laura and Donna from the communications team head to Romania for the filming of Sainsbury's Dickensian-themed Christmas advert. How will it stand up against the commercials produced by their rivals?(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.title: Election 2019
programme.desc: As the polls close and the overnight count begins, Mark Carruthers and a panel of political experts analyse the results from Northern Ireland's 18 Westminster constituencies as well as the emerging political picture across England, Scotland and Wales. programme.desc: Huw Edwards takes the helm for the BBC's comprehensive overnight coverage as the results of the General Election are announced, joined by Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Faisal Islam and Amol Rajan, along with Professor John Curtice. Sophie Raworth analyses the results on a giant map of the UK, Sarah Smith and Kirsty Wark broadcast live from Scotland, and correspondents including Naga Munchetty, Andrew Marr, Martha Kearney, Nick Robinson and Lucy Manning will have news and results from key constituency locations across the country. programme.title: Air Tòir Manachainn Dhèir (The Lost Monastery of Deer)
programme.desc: One of the most important texts in the Scottish Gaelic language, the Book of Deer was written in the ancient monastery of Deer that disappeared over a thousand years ago. This is the story of a team of archeologists and volunteers who spent nine days hunting for the lost monastery of Deer, where the book was written. In the event, those involved found more than they imagined possible(n)

However, surgeons give new hope to a young girl with extremely bowed legs due to malnutrition, a teenage boy with a burns injury so severe his toes don't touch the ground, and a local paediatrician in need of novel and complex surgery so he can get back to work and continue to save the lives of his people(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. programme.desc: Aaron's confident that his campaign will work, while Archie is pushed to his limit and lashes out at Angelica after she and Arthur make fun of him. When Jimmy yanks Archie away, an angry Jai tells Jimmy it's nowhere near over between them(n)

programme.title: The Royal Variety Performance
programme.desc: Rob Beckett and Romesh Ranganathan host the annual entertainment extravaganza from the London Palladium, attended by the Duke and Duchess of Cambridge. The show features music from Robbie Williams, Jamie Cullum, Emeli Sande, Lewis Capaldi, the Kanneh Masons and Mabel, as well as this year's Britain's Got Talent winner, Colin Thackery, and comedy from Frank Skinner, Kerry Godliman and Flo & Joan. There are also appearances by the casts of West End shows Groan Ups, Mary Poppins and Come from Away, and dance spectacle from Zurcaroh and Cirque du Soleil(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: Jonathan is joined by guests including The Grand Tour and Who Wants To Be A Millionaire? presenter Jeremy Clarkson, BAFTA-nominated actor Samson Kayo, two-time Oscar winner Glenda Jackson and This Morning and Dancing on Ice presenter Holly Willoughby. Plus, Stereophonics will be performing in the studio(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Bentley, mechanic David, army paramedic Nick and dancer Josh enter the 'love lift' in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date - but must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Host Paddy McGuinness presides over the matching process, and also catches up on the latest developments from all the couples in Fernando's as the dating game bows out for another series(n)

programme.title: The Switch

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: The X Factor: The Band
programme.desc: Music mogul Simon Cowell continues his search for the next big group to sign to his record label and follow in the footsteps of One Direction, Fifth Harmony and Little Mix. He knows that singing groups discovered on The X Factor have sold more than 400 million records, so he is hoping he can find another who can hopefully be just as successful - if not more so(n)

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with members of Westminster, topical guests and cultural figures. With updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key-players throughout the programme(n)

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Tom Dove and Neil Spooner head to Italy and travel to a lake in the hills near Bologna, where they have just two days to catch a whopper. Then it is off for the main event at a large water called Viverone, not far from the Alps, where they go up against fish with a reputation for outwitting anglers(n)

programme.title: Animal Mums
programme.desc: How young animals copy their mother to learn new skills that are vital to keep them a live in the wild, including cheetah cubs learning how to hunt, with Liesl Eichenberger of the Sanbona Reserve in South Africa explaining the different stages of hunt school. Gorilla researcher Eva Maria Luef reveals the different gestures used to beg for food from their mother and initiate play, while Ellen Jackson at a stud farm in Sacramento talks about the close bond between horse foals and their mother, and how they learn to socialise and run with the herd. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo begins this week in the Italian food capital of Bologna, where he picks up a snack for his journey - the Bolognese classic panino with mortadella. He then boards the train and heads towards the rice growing region of Mantova, where he helps out in the paddies and makes a chocolate rice pudding. He then visits Verona, home of one of the country's oldest art schools. Here he models for the students in their life-drawing class before serving up meatballs in tomato sauce for the young artists(n)

programme.title: Election 2019 Live
programme.desc: As the polls close, News at Ten anchor Tom Bradby presents live through-the-night coverage of the battle for Number 10, revealing who will lead the country for the next five years. He is joined in the studio by his ITV colleagues and two political analysts - Professor Jane Green, co-director of the British Election Study, and election analyst Professor Colin Rallings. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Veteran singer and keyboard player Alan Price guest stars in this festive episode of the rural drama, as the leader of a jazz band. Travelling to Aidensfield, where they've been booked by Vernon to play at the villagers' Christmas concert, the Rio Trio offer a lift to a girl, unaware she is a single mum taking her baby home to see her parents for the holiday. However, the first the band know about the infant is when the girl disappears, leaving them to look after it. Meanwhile, as the concert gets into full swing, local barmaid Gina takes centre stage, hoping to impress all and sundry with her rendition of Winter Wonderland. Tricia Penrose, Derek Fowlds, Geoffrey Hughes, William Simons and Mark Jordon star(n)

programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the cloistered world of Midsomer Priory when a nun is found strangled. The detectives uncover a complex mystery involving holy orders, teenage romantic liaisons, African art and missing antique silver as the community is forced to open its doors to 21st-century policing. Former EastEnders actor Cliff Parisi (Minty) guest stars alongside Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: Returning for a second attempt at the Hatton Garden safe deposit vault, the gang members, now led by Terry Perkins, set out to finish what they started. They must obtain new equipment and get back into the building, but time is getting tight with the bank holiday weekend slipping by. Drama, starring Timothy Spall and Kenneth Cranham(n)

programme.title: In Plain Sight
programme.desc: Muncie is convinced Manuel has now committed four murders, but William Watt is arrested on suspicion of killing his wife, daughter and sister-in-law, despite having been on a fishing trip at the time. With no arrest made following Anne Kneiland's murder, Muncie is determined to get the truth and visits Manuel's father, who has given his son the alibi he needed to be free of police suspicion. While Muncie finally casts enough suspicion on Watt's arrest to have him released, it is not long before another young girl goes missing(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey(n)

She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Young physiotherapist Lizzie Faulkner is shot dead while enjoying a holiday at a luxury country retreat. The resulting murder investigation unearths betrayal, guilt and the desire for atonement - as Vera moves quickly to avoid another tragedy, and Joe tries to make amends with the demons of his past. Crime drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, David Leon, Paul Ritter and Eva Quinn(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: When the Metropolitan Police discover the burglary after the long Easter weekend, the gang members already have a two-day lead on them. They are desperate to split up the stolen goods, but more delays frustrate them. However, they soon come to realise that it is not only the police who are chasing them - Brian Reader also wants his share(n)

programme.title: In Plain Sight
programme.desc: The day after Isabelle Cooke disappears, Manuel hands himself in to Muncie with an alibi for his movements of the previous 24 hours, leaving him free once again. Four days later Peter and Doris Smart and their 12-year-old son are shot dead in their beds. The killer takes care to dispose of their bodies and the gun used to do the deed, but also pockets 15 one-pound notes found in Peter Smart's wallet. On discovering that the previously flat-broke Manuel had been generously buying rounds of drinks on New Year's Day, Muncie begins to put the pieces of the puzzle together, and begins to close in on the Beast of Birkenshaw(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The surly detective tries to get out of attending a reunion of George Cross holders because a serial rapist has struck again on his patch. Righteous indignation prompts him to take the most desperate professional gamble of his entire career by using PC Hazel Wallace as bait in a trap. He eventually picks up a lead involving the most recent victim's father, but comes under pressure from within the force to back down or be thrown off the case. David Jason stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the cloistered world of Midsomer Priory when a nun is found strangled. The detectives uncover a complex mystery involving holy orders, teenage romantic liaisons, African art and missing antique silver as the community is forced to open its doors to 21st-century policing. Former EastEnders actor Cliff Parisi (Minty) guest stars alongside Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: Returning for a second attempt at the Hatton Garden safe deposit vault, the gang members, now led by Terry Perkins, set out to finish what they started. They must obtain new equipment and get back into the building, but time is getting tight with the bank holiday weekend slipping by. Drama, starring Timothy Spall and Kenneth Cranham(n)

programme.title: In Plain Sight
programme.desc: Muncie is convinced Manuel has now committed four murders, but William Watt is arrested on suspicion of killing his wife, daughter and sister-in-law, despite having been on a fishing trip at the time. With no arrest made following Anne Kneiland's murder, Muncie is determined to get the truth and visits Manuel's father, who has given his son the alibi he needed to be free of police suspicion. While Muncie finally casts enough suspicion on Watt's arrest to have him released, it is not long before another young girl goes missing(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Young physiotherapist Lizzie Faulkner is shot dead while enjoying a holiday at a luxury country retreat. The resulting murder investigation unearths betrayal, guilt and the desire for atonement - as Vera moves quickly to avoid another tragedy, and Joe tries to make amends with the demons of his past. Crime drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, David Leon, Paul Ritter and Eva Quinn(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: When the Metropolitan Police discover the burglary after the long Easter weekend, the gang members already have a two-day lead on them. They are desperate to split up the stolen goods, but more delays frustrate them. However, they soon come to realise that it is not only the police who are chasing them - Brian Reader also wants his share(n)

programme.title: In Plain Sight
programme.desc: The day after Isabelle Cooke disappears, Manuel hands himself in to Muncie with an alibi for his movements of the previous 24 hours, leaving him free once again. Four days later Peter and Doris Smart and their 12-year-old son are shot dead in their beds. The killer takes care to dispose of their bodies and the gun used to do the deed, but also pockets 15 one-pound notes found in Peter Smart's wallet. On discovering that the previously flat-broke Manuel had been generously buying rounds of drinks on New Year's Day, Muncie begins to put the pieces of the puzzle together, and begins to close in on the Beast of Birkenshaw(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The surly detective tries to get out of attending a reunion of George Cross holders because a serial rapist has struck again on his patch. Righteous indignation prompts him to take the most desperate professional gamble of his entire career by using PC Hazel Wallace as bait in a trap. He eventually picks up a lead involving the most recent victim's father, but comes under pressure from within the force to back down or be thrown off the case. David Jason stars(n)

programme.desc: Donna is trapped underneath the tractor, Seth learns that Alan may sell the game farm, and Nick and Elsa see the judge to decide Alice's future(n)

programme.desc: Charlie warns Reg to keep his mouth shut, Steve tells Mike he cannot manage all the bills and asks if they can renegotiate terms. Rita offers Sally financial help(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: New broom Sgt Miller (John Duttine) introduces a rapid response car but his big city ideas receive a cool reception from the Aidensfield watch. Walker is beaten up by hoodlums but falls for nurse Clare Owen during his stay in hospital(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Respected surgeon Dan Marsden is shot dead days before his retirement and his teenage daughters Mira and Karen are abducted. Joined by a new addition to the ranks, Vera investigates a mysterious parcel and a connection to the Iraq War as she races against time to catch the killer and save the girls. However, the case soon takes a violent and unexpected turn, striking at the heart of the team. Guest starring Saskia Reeves (Luther) and Dean Andrews (Ashes to Ashes), with Brenda Blethyn, David Leon and Gareth Farr(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: With the surveillance teams hot on their tails, the gang members squabble among themselves over who should receive a cut of the takings. Having performed such a professional raid, they are now behaving like amateurs, and with tensions running high, Perkins breaks his own golden rule by organising another meeting. Will he cut Reader in on the deal? Timothy Spall, Kenneth Cranham and David Hayman star in the fact-based drama(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: A foreign spy trained to kill and impersonate a top Canadian agent presents a problem for both the team and his employers. Crime drama, starring Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar investigates the murder of a journalist friend, following a trail of clues that leads to a cult obsessed with the splendour and savagery of ancient Rome. Drama, guest starring Peter Wyngarde and Ed Bishop(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A sailor is murdered shortly after arriving in the US from Greece, and Theo is convinced he smuggled something into the country - a stamp that is valuable enough to kill for(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The bodies of typhoid victims washed up by flood waters threaten to contaminate water supplies in Los Angeles, leaving Quincy fighting to avert a major health scare(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur becomes involved with a gambler, whose sudden death leaves the dodgy car dealer £2,000 worse off. Meanwhile, Terry helps his old flame Sylvie leave her abusive husband. Billy Connolly guest stars in the vintage comedy drama, alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan persuades a villain to act as an informant providing evidence against his two accomplices. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman, and featuring an early screen appearance by Christopher Ellison(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A crooked foreign diplomat helps arrange the theft of a consignment of silver bullion being transported through Tilbury docks. Bodie and Doyle are assigned to investigate, and it becomes apparent that they have a useful link to the villains via Doyle's girlfriend Deborah. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar is suspected of involvement in a series of jewel robberies on the French Riviera, and has to stay one step ahead of the police long enough to catch the real culprit. Guest starring Ronnie Barker and Aimee Delamain(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A woman whom Theo had helped to kick a drug habit seemingly commits suicide, but the detective is convinced she was murdered. Guest starring John Glover (Smallville)(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: After a jet liner crashes just outside of Los Angeles, Quincy believes sabotage may caused the major air disaster, but finds proving his suspicion is no easy task(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A tape implicating two senior police officers in a criminal conspiracy is mistakenly delivered to Daley Videos, and Arthur and Terry find themselves placed in contact with its owner - a local major crime boss. Ian McShane, Rula Lenska, Brian Blessed and Ray Winstone guest star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie works alongside an old friend who once saved his life while serving in the SAS, and has difficulty coping with mounting evidence that his ex-colleague has joined the gang of Italian terrorists they are shadowing. Crime drama, starring Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Recently released criminal Eddie Boyse becomes prime suspect in an assault case - and Regan believes he is guilty. Unfortunately for the officer, Boyse has the perfect alibi - he was in Regan's company when the crime was committed. Drama, starring John Thaw, with Dennis Waterman and Del Henney(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney visit three diverse venues across England. They first target barbel on the River Trent alongside Matt Godfrey, before switching their attention to carp on the Grand Union Canal, and finally teaming up with Tom Dove and Steve Ringer at Earlswood Lake(n)

programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle infiltrate a gang smuggling art treasures and industrial secrets out of the country and selling them on to foreign powers. programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: Road Trip
programme.desc: The final destination on the trio's culinary adventure is Gordon Ramsay's birthplace of Scotland, where the chef is planning a feast for the chief of Clan Ramsay and his esteemed friends. If the meal is successful Gordon will be officially welcomed into the clan, so he is desperate for everything to be perfect. The trio set out to tour the country in search of ingredients, including Scottish beef and scallops, but also find time to take part in the Highland Games and dress up like real Scotsmen - with Gino taking a particular shine to his sporran(n)

And an entrepreneur has a gadget to create the perfect Christmas hairdo, with a new take on the blow dry. Presented by Rylan Clark-Neal(n)

programme.title: Christmas at Chatsworth House
programme.desc: One-off documentary going behind the scenes of Chatsworth, one of England's most impressive stately homes, as the Duke, Duchess and their 800 staff brace themselves for an influx of 200,000 visitors over the 2019 festive season. Christmas is the most important event for the Derbyshire estate and crucial to its survival. In just five days, the house is transformed with 30 Christmas trees, 60,000 baubles, and a million fairy lights. But this year Derbyshire has been crippled by unprecedented flooding. Can family and staff rally to make this a season to be remembered?(n)

programme.title: Channel 4's Alternative Election Night
programme.desc: Krishnan Guru-Murthy and Katherine Ryan host a light-hearted take on polling day and the General Election, with Rylan Clark-Neal getting the thoughts of the studio audience. Clare Balding has the results as they happen, Matt Forde will run the Alternative News Desk and Rob Rinder is out and about assessing the mood on the ground. Throughout the night the presenters are joined by a mixture of political figures and satirists - including ex-Home Secretary Amber Rudd, former Labour Deputy Leader Tom Watson and comedians Tez Ilyas and Sophie Willan - adding a comedic slant to the analysis. Channel 4 News Political Editor Gary Gibbon will have his ear to the ground bringing breaking news and a team of correspondents will be stationed at the most important counts to assess exactly what is going on up and down the country(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Steam Railway: All Aboard
programme.desc: A summer heatwave brings record numbers to the railway, though joy turns to despair for the staff when it looks like the railway will be prevented from running its iconic steam engines through a tinder-dry national park. Through a series of expensive preventative measures - including hiring in a couple of heritage diesel trains and having a makeshift fire truck follow every engine - boss Chris manages to keep the business running. The engine shed workers are also kept busy trying to get star locomotive Eric steaming again(n)

programme.title: Great British Ships
programme.desc: Rob Bell reveals the secrets of ill-fated liner the Titanic, whose maiden voyage became the most famous maritime disaster in history. In the Belfast shipyard where the Titanic was built, Rob explores how the city took on the daunting challenge of creating such a gigantic ship, and as the tragic story unfolds, he demonstrates why its groundbreaking safety systems ultimately failed, costing the lives of more than 1,500 people(n)

programme.title: Hoarders: Landfill in My Living Room
programme.desc: Documentary series following people with a compulsion to hoard. The first episode features a woman diagnosed with cancer whose home is full of rotten food and dead mice, a father whose relationship with his daughter has suffered as a result of his obsession, and a Huddersfield man whose clutter caught fire, burning his house to the ground(n)

programme.title: Greatest Christmas TV Moments
programme.desc: Countdown of 50 festive crackers past and present, including classic soap moments, dramatic endings, sitcom specials, comedy sketches and light entertainment highlights that have made the television set the focus of the holiday season for decades. Highlights include famous couples Den and Angie, Jack and Vera, and Gavin & Stacey; popular double acts Morecambe and Wise, the Trotter brothers and Wallace and Gromit; Doctor Who meeting Kylie Minogue; perennial music show Top of the Pops; and the ultimate irony of sitting down to watch the Royle Family sit down to watch TV for an hour. All small-screen life is here - with jingle bells on. Featuring interviews with cast, crew and assorted celebrity contributors(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Steam Railway: All Aboard
programme.desc: Preparations are under way for Yorkshire Day, including a special Yorkshire-themed Pullman service, with Gill and husband Paul getting hands-on sorting out the event. There is also another big occasion on the horizon and Tim is organising the wedding of head boilersmith Mark and his equally steam-mad fiancée Emma. Their love blossomed because of mutual passion for steam, and in particular the B1, so they want the engine to pull them on their wedding train, but issues with the engine means it might miss the big day(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Your Supermarket Food
programme.desc: Sian Williams and Stefan Gates lift the lid on the food that goes into the annual festive feast. Stefan visits a turkey farm to investigate the reason for the price difference for a basic white turkey, at around £20, and a Norfolk Black, which can cost £75. Sian discovers how the bitter taste is being bred out of sprouts and there's a report on mince pies, revealing how the most expensive may not be the best tasting. Plus, why do people fall asleep after eating Christmas dinner?(n)

programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the inner workings of the supermarket during the festive period, revealing the secrets of the retailer's success, and how it rose from a grocery chain that once baffled its UK customers, to a brand that took almost one billion pounds in December 2018, enjoying its busiest week to date. The programme also follows the retailer from its arrival in Britain in the 1990s, through changing times and changing tastes to become the brand it is today, and reveals how the discounter came to fill television screens at Christmas, taking on the big name brand adverts with their own festive versions(n)

programme.title: It's Your Fault I'm Fat
programme.desc: Cameras focus on 18-year-old Charlotte, who lives near Southampton and weighs 25st. She is desperate to shed her own weight, and also fears her dad's size could be life-threatening(n)

programme.title: Gypsy Kids: Our Secret World
programme.desc: Nine-year-old traveller Jack Joe has decides he is old enough to move out of the family chalet and into his own trailer. To mark his independence, his father is letting him buy a three-wheeled motorbike - and to conduct the deal by himself. Meanwhile, hundreds of travellers gather for the lavish funeral of Doherty family patriarch Simey, including Francie, Johnny and Margaret, just three of his 150-plus grandchildren and great-grandchildren(n)

programme.title: Heists
programme.desc: On a cold November morning in 1983, armed robbers targeted the Brinks Mat security depot at the Heathrow International Trading Estate. Following an extremely brutal military-style raid, the gang got away with over £26million of gold. The robbery - the country's biggest and most notorious - caused the price of gold to soar and sent ripples through the criminal underworld. This programme hears from police officers who followed the increasingly complex case, as it pieces together the legacy of the heist(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Steam Railway: All Aboard
programme.desc: The annual Gala Festival is staged, showcasing steam engines from up and down the country. As part of the events, the North York Moors Railway has agreed to swap its precious 100-year-old Q6 engine, the last example in the world, with an engine from the Severn Valley heritage railway. However, there is a problem as the Q6 is undergoing a two-year overhaul and is not ready. A crack has been found in the engine box, and there is no specialist steam copper welder available at short notice to fix it. If the Q6 cannot be sent to the Severn Valley, the entire festival may be ruined(n)

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild UK
programme.desc: Ben meets 40-year-old Amanda Owen from Huddersfield, who left behind a comfortable existence to pursue her lifelong dream of becoming a hill shepherdess. Despite having no background in farming, she now lives at a 2,000-acre farm in the Yorkshire Dales with husband Clive and their eight children. Ben is given a taste of life as a hill shepherd, learns the highs and lows of farming life and finds out how she copes looking after such a huge family as well as her 1,000 native Swaledale sheep(n)

programme.title: Make a Wish for Christmas
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary about Make-A-Wish UK, a charity that grants wishes to children with life-threatening conditions. The dedicated team work in a small office block in Reading, helping children enjoy all manner of experiences, from visiting Milan fashion week and sleeping with tigers to sharing their lunch with their favourite football team and meeting Santa in Lapland(n)

programme.title: Most Shocking Celebrity Moments
programme.desc: A countdown of 40 of the most shocking celebrity antics of the year. All sorts of bad behaviour is covered from Kanye West's outbursts to Ant McPartlin's drink-driving incident. Other stories include Madonna's off-colour tribute to Aretha Franklin at the VMAs and the most shocking moments from Celebrity Big Brother. Plus, the tense stand-off between Kim Woodburn and Coleen Nolan on Loose Women, President Trump's controversial UK visit and claims concerning David and Victoria Beckham's marriage. Featuring contributions by Louis Walsh, Jeremy Vine, Michelle Visage, Jermaine Pennant, Dan Osborne, India Willoughby and Courtney Act(n)

programme.title: Arabesque (1966)
programme.desc: An American professor working at Oxford University is hired by a mysterious Arabic businessman to decipher some ancient hieroglyphics. When he learns they contain a message linked to a planned political assassination, he is drawn into a series of escapades with his employer's henchmen in hot pursuit. Spy thriller, starring Gregory Peck, Sophia Loren, Alan Badel and Kieron Moore(n)

programme.title: Man in the Saddle (1951)
programme.desc: A wealthy and ambitious cattleman plans to seize all the land surrounding his own. A peaceful rancher living nearby ignores warnings to sell up and avoid a conflict, and as he suffers a campaign of intimidation, he is forced to adopt violent methods to defend his livelihood. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Alexander Knox, Joan Leslie and Ellen Drew(n)

programme.title: The Return of the Musketeers (1989)
programme.desc: D'Artagnan reunites his former companions to save the queen of France from the scheming Cardinal Mazarin, and embark on a mission to England to prevent the beheading of Charles I. Meanwhile, the daughter of a former vanquished enemy is seeking revenge on the four heroes. Swashbuckling adventure sequel, with Oliver Reed, Michael York, Frank Finlay, Richard Chamberlain, Kim Cattrall and Roy Kinnear, who died during filming(n)

programme.title: The Man from the Alamo (1953)
programme.desc: A soldier serving at the battle of the Alamo is sent to warn his comrades' families of the impending danger, but ends up falsely branded a deserter. He tries to clear his name with acts of heroism, including tracking down the traitor who robbed the casualties after the battle was over. Western, starring Glenn Ford and Julia Adams(n)

programme.title: The Monuments Men (2014)
programme.desc: Seven experts in fine art are assembled into a military platoon during the Second World War, and given an unusual mission. They are sent into occupied Europe to recover priceless works of art that have fallen into Nazi hands and ensure the masterpieces survive the conflict. Fact-based drama, directed by and starring George Clooney. With Matt Damon, Bill Murray and Cate Blanchett(n)

programme.title: A Good Day to Die Hard (2013)
programme.desc: John McClane goes to Russia having received word that his estranged son Jack is in trouble with the law. He is surprised to discover Jack is secretly a CIA agent, and father and son join forces to take on the Russian Mob, who are engaged in a plot to steal nuclear weapons. Action adventure sequel, starring Bruce Willis and Jai Courtney(n)

programme.title: The Purge: Anarchy (2014)
programme.desc: A woman, her daughter and a couple are lost on the streets during a dangerous annual night when all laws are suspended. A gun-toting loner comes to their aid and fights to protect them, while also plotting to take advantage of the situation to pursue a murderous mission of his own. Horror sequel, starring Frank Grillo and Carmen Ejogo(n)

programme.title: The Principles of Lust (2003)
programme.desc: An aspiring artist is faced with a dilemma when he is forced to choose between a normal family life with his girlfriend and her son, or a hedonistic whirl of sex, drugs and rock 'n' roll with a risk-taking friend. Drama, starring Alec Newman, Marc Warren, Sienna Guillory and Lara Clifton(n)

programme.title: The Lawless Breed (1953)
programme.desc: A gunslinger newly released from prison tells the story of his eventful life to a newspaper reporter in the hope of salvaging his brutal reputation. Fact-based Western, starring Rock Hudson and Julie Adams(n)

Adaptation of the classic adventure tales, starring Jon Hall, Maria Montez, Sabu and Leif Erickson(n)

Carol Reed's satirical spy drama, based on Graham Greene's novel, starring Alec Guinness, Ralph Richardson, Maureen O'Hara, Noel Coward and Burl Ives(n)

The king of Sparta steps forward to defend his homeland and the other kingdoms of Greece, but when he is betrayed by a political council, he finds himself without an army and must fend off the vast invading army with a mere 300 warriors. Historical drama, starring Richard Egan, Ralph Richardson and Diane Baker(n)

Comedy action thriller, starring Ryan Reynolds, Samuel L Jackson, Gary Oldman and Salma Hayek(n)

programme.title: The Purge: Election Year (2016)

Action horror sequel, starring Frank Grillo, Mykelti Williamson and Elizabeth Mitchell(n)

Comedy drama anthology, starring Dario Grandinetti and Maria Marull. In Spanish(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
